reasons: Tigreal can group and stun enemies, giving Balmond a clear area to enter with Cyclone Sweep.
Balmond can follow Tigreal's engage and save Lethal Counter for enemies already lowered by the teamfight.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates the crowd-control opening, and Balmond follows into the clustered fight with close-range AoE damage and execute pressure.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal lands the engage before Balmond commits.
Balmond enters while enemies are still grouped.
Lethal Counter is saved for low-HP targets.
failureCases: Tigreal's engage is interrupted or misses.
Balmond arrives after the control window ends.
The team lacks damage to put enemies into execute range.

reasons: Atlas can pull multiple enemies into one area, giving Balmond a strong Cyclone Sweep target zone.
Balmond can use the grouped fight to pressure several enemies and look for Lethal Counter finishes.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies through hard engage, while Balmond converts the cluster into sustained close-range damage and execute threat.
worksBestWhen: Atlas catches multiple enemies near an objective or chokepoint.
Balmond is already close enough to follow.
The team focuses the pulled targets during Balmond's spin.
failureCases: Atlas catches only a low-value target.
Balmond is kited before reaching the pulled area.
Enemy cleanse, immunity, or disengage breaks the chain timing.

reasons: Angela can attach to Balmond and shield him during his entry into the enemy formation.
Balmond can use the extra protection to keep spinning near priority targets and prepare Lethal Counter.

proof.summary: Balmond commits into close range, and Angela's Heartguard can add protection during that commitment.
worksBestWhen: Angela shields Balmond as he starts the dive.
Balmond uses the shield window to stay near enemies.
The team follows up while Balmond is drawing pressure.
failureCases: Angela is caught before casting Heartguard.
Balmond dives too far from follow-up damage.
Enemy burst or anti-heal reduces the value of the support window.

reasons: Estes can keep Balmond healthy during sustained melee fights.
Balmond benefits when the team stays grouped and lets Estes maintain healing while he frontlines.

proof.summary: Balmond wants to stay inside the fight, and Estes can extend that frontline presence with repeated healing.
worksBestWhen: Balmond stays inside Estes' healing range.
The fight lasts long enough for healing to matter.
Estes is protected from enemy dive.
failureCases: Anti-heal cuts down the sustain value.
Burst damage removes Estes or Balmond before the fight extends.

reasons: Rafaela gives Balmond movement speed and healing, helping him stay near targets during Cyclone Sweep.
Her support helps Balmond chase, reset position, or continue brawling after entry.

proof.summary: Balmond needs to keep contact with enemies, while Rafaela's speed and healing help him maintain pressure in skirmishes.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela uses her speed boost as Balmond enters or chases.
The team uses the speed window to follow the fight.
failureCases: Balmond is stopped by hard crowd control before reaching targets.
Enemies use repeated dashes or disengage.
Rafaela is forced away from Balmond's path.
